- How outputs are post-processed into text

Be thorough - read the actual code of at least 2-3 existing pipeline implementations that involve both images and text.

Explore the Transformers.js codebase to understand how models are registered and used. I need to plan a new `image-text-to-text` pipeline that uses `AutoModelForImageTextToText`.

Please find and read:
1. The AutoModel classes - look for AutoModelForImageTextToText or similar image-text models (in src/models/ or src/models.js)
2. The task-to-pipeline mapping (look for SUPPORTED_TASKS, task_mapping, or similar)
3. How other "ForCausalLM" or text generation models work in pipelines (generate method, tokenizer usage)
4. Look for any existing image-text-to-text or vision-language model implementations (like LLaVA, PaliGemma, Qwen-VL, etc.)
5. The exports/index file to understand what gets exported

Focus on:
- AutoModelForImageTextToText class definition and what it extends
- The generate() method pattern in generation pipelines
- How models handle multimodal inputs (pixel_values + input_ids)
- The TASK_ALIASES or task name registration patterns

393	    /** @type {TokenClassificationPipelineCallback} */
394	    async _call(texts, {

396	    } = {}) {
397	
398	        const isBatched = Array.isArray(texts);
399	
400	        // Run tokenization
401	        const model_inputs = this.tokenizer(isBatched ? texts : [texts], {
402	            padding: true,
403	            truncation: true,
404	        });
405	
406	        // Run model
407	        const outputs = await this.model(model_inputs)
408	
409	        const logits = outputs.logits;
410	        // @ts-expect-error TS2339
411	        const id2label = this.model.config.id2label;
412	
413	        const toReturn = [];
414	        for (let i = 0; i < logits.dims[0]; ++i) {
415	            const ids = model_inputs.input_ids[i];
416	            const batch = logits[i];
417	
418	            // List of tokens that aren't ignored
419	            const tokens = [];
420	            for (let j = 0; j < batch.dims[0]; ++j) {
421	                const tokenData = batch[j];
422	                const topScoreIndex = max(tokenData.data)[1];
423	
424	                const entity = id2label ? id2label[topScoreIndex] : `LABEL_${topScoreIndex}`;
425	                if (ignore_labels.includes(entity)) {
426	                    // We predicted a token that should be ignored. So, we skip it.
427	                    continue;
428	                }
429	
430	                // TODO add option to keep special tokens?
431	                const word = this.tokenizer.decode([ids[j].item()], { skip_special_tokens: true });
432	                if (word === '') {
433	                    // Was a special token. So, we skip it.
434	                    continue;
435	                }
436	
437	                const scores = softmax(tokenData.data);
438	
439	                tokens.push({
440	                    entity: entity,
441	                    score: scores[topScoreIndex],
442	                    index: j,
443	                    word: word,
444	
445	                    // TODO: Add support for start and end
446	                    // start: null,
447	                    // end: null,
448	                });
449	            }
450	            toReturn.push(tokens);
451	        }
452	        return isBatched ? toReturn : toReturn[0];

1990	
1991	        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImages);
1992

/**
 * The Pipeline class is the class from which all pipelines inherit.
 * Refer to this class for methods shared across different pipelines.
 */
export class Pipeline extends Callable {
    /**
     * Create a new Pipeline.
     * @param {Object} options An object containing the following properties:
     * @param {string} [options.task] The task of the pipeline. Useful for specifying subtasks.
     * @param {PreTrainedModel} [options.model] The model used by the pipeline.
     * @param {PreTrainedTokenizer} [options.tokenizer=null] The tokenizer used by the pipeline (if any).
     * @param {Processor} [options.processor=null] The processor used by the pipeline (if any).
     */
    constructor({ task, model, tokenizer = null, processor = null }) {
        super();
        this.task = task;
        this.model = model;
        this.tokenizer = tokenizer;
        this.processor = processor;
    }

    /** @type {DisposeType} */
    async dispose() {
        await this.model.dispose();
    }
}

/**
 * @typedef {Object} ModelTokenizerConstructorArgs
 * @property {string} task The task of the pipeline. Useful for specifying subtasks.
 * @property {PreTrainedModel} model The model used by the pipeline.
 * @property {PreTrainedTokenizer} tokenizer The tokenizer used by the pipeline.
 *
 * @typedef {ModelTokenizerConstructorArgs} TextPipelineConstructorArgs An object used to instantiate a text-based pipeline.
 */

/**
 * @typedef {Object} ModelProcessorConstructorArgs
 * @property {string} task The task of the pipeline. Useful for specifying subtasks.
 * @property {PreTrainedModel} model The model used by the pipeline.
 * @property {Processor} processor The processor used by the pipeline.
 *
 * @typedef {ModelProcessorConstructorArgs} AudioPipelineConstructorArgs An object used to instantiate an audio-based pipeline.
 * @typedef {ModelProcessorConstructorArgs} ImagePipelineConstructorArgs An object used to instantiate an image-based pipeline.
 */


/**
 * @typedef {Object} ModelTokenizerProcessorConstructorArgs
 * @property {string} task The task of the pipeline. Useful for specifying subtasks.
 * @property {PreTrainedModel} model The model used by the pipeline.
 * @property {PreTrainedTokenizer} tokenizer The tokenizer used by the pipeline.
 * @property {Processor} processor The processor used by the pipeline.
 *
 * @typedef {ModelTokenizerProcessorConstructorArgs} TextAudioPipelineConstructorArgs An object used to instantiate a text- and audio-based pipeline.
 * @typedef {ModelTokenizerProcessorConstructorArgs} TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs An object used to instantiate a text- and image-based pipeline.
 */

/**

# Plan: Add `image-text-to-text` Pipeline to Transformers.js

## Context

Transformers.js already has `AutoModelForImageTextToText` (in `src/models.js`) with full
generation support for models like LLaVA, PaliGemma, Qwen2-VL, Moondream1, Florence2, and
Idefics3. However, the `pipeline()` factory function has **no** `"image-text-to-text"` entry in
`SUPPORTED_TASKS`, so end-users cannot access these models through the high-level API.

The goal is to add the missing pipeline class `ImageTextToTextPipeline` and register it in
`SUPPORTED_TASKS` — following patterns already established by `ImageToTextPipeline` and
`DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline`, without touching any existing class.

---

## Critical File

**Only one file needs to be modified:** `src/pipelines.js`

The main export barrel (`src/transformers.js`) already does `export * from './pipelines.js'`, so
the new class and task become available automatically.

---

## Existing Utilities to Reuse

| Utility | Location | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `prepareImages()` | `src/pipelines.js` line 91 | Normalises single/array image inputs into `RawImage[]` |
| `TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs` typedef | `src/pipelines.js` line 215 | Type for pipelines that need tokenizer + processor + model |
| `AutoTokenizer` | already imported at line 17 | Text tokenizer (decode generated token ids) |
| `AutoProcessor` | already imported at line 47 | Handles combined image+text preprocessing |
| `AutoModelForImageTextToText` | `src/models.js` line 8736 | **Needs to be added to existing import block** |

---

## Step-by-Step Changes

### Step 1 — Add `AutoModelForImageTextToText` to the import block

**Location:** `src/pipelines.js` lines 16–45 (the `import { ... } from './models.js'` block)

Add `AutoModelForImageTextToText` to the existing named-import list, alongside the other
`AutoModelFor*` classes. Alphabetical placement: after `AutoModelForImageFeatureExtraction`,
before `AutoModelForImageToImage`.

```js
// existing import block – just add this one name:
AutoModelForImageTextToText,
```

---

### Step 2 — Add JSDoc typedefs (insert after line 1953)

Insert immediately **after** the `ImageToTextPipelineType` typedef block (which ends at line
1953) and **before** the `ImageToTextPipeline` class doc-comment (line 1956):

```js
/**
 * @typedef {Object} ImageTextToTextSingle
 * @property {string} generated_text The generated text.
 * @typedef {ImageTextToTextSingle[]} ImageTextToTextOutput
 *
 * @callback ImageTextToTextPipelineCallback Run a generative language model on the concatenation of an image and text prompt.
 * @param {ImagePipelineInputs} images The image(s) to use as visual context.
 * @param {string|string[]} text The text prompt(s) to use for generation.
 * @param {Partial<import('./generation/configuration_utils.js').GenerationConfig>} [options] Additional keyword arguments passed to the model's generate method.
 * @returns {Promise<ImageTextToTextOutput|ImageTextToTextOutput[]>} An object (or array of objects) containing the generated text(s).
 *
 * @typedef {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs & ImageTextToTextPipelineCallback & Disposable} ImageTextToTextPipelineType
 */
```

---

### Step 3 — Add `ImageTextToTextPipeline` class (insert after line 2005)

Insert immediately **after** the closing brace of `ImageToTextPipeline` (line 2005), before the
`ImageClassificationSingle` typedef block.

```js
/**
 * Image-text-to-text pipeline using `AutoModelForImageTextToText`. This pipeline generates
 * text from an image and a text prompt, enabling tasks such as visual question answering,
 * image captioning with prompts, and visual chat.
 *
 * **Example:** Answer a visual question w/ `HuggingFaceTB/SmolVLM-256M-Instruct`.
 * ```javascript
 * const pipe = await pipeline('image-text-to-text', 'HuggingFaceTB/SmolVLM-256M-Instruct');
 * const image = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/cats.jpg';
 * const text = 'What do you see in this image?';
 * const output = await pipe(image, text);
 * // [{ generated_text: 'Two cats are lying on a couch.' }]
 * ```
 */
export class ImageTextToTextPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs) => ImageTextToTextPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {

    /**
     * Create a new ImageTextToTextPipeline.
     * @param {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs} options An object used to instantiate the pipeline.
     */
    constructor(options) {
        super(options);
    }

    /** @type {ImageTextToTextPipelineCallback} */
    async _call(images, text, generate_kwargs = {}) {
        const isBatched = Array.isArray(images);
        const preparedImages = await prepareImages(images);
        const texts = Array.isArray(text) ? text : preparedImages.map(() => text);

        const toReturn = [];
        for (let i = 0; i < preparedImages.length; i++) {
            const inputs = await this.processor(preparedImages[i], texts[i]);
            const output = await this.model.generate({ ...inputs, ...generate_kwargs });
            const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output), {
                skip_special_tokens: true,
            }).map(x => ({ generated_text: x.trim() }));
            toReturn.push(decoded);
        }

        return isBatched ? toReturn : toReturn[0];
    }
}
```

**Key design decisions:**
- Images and their corresponding texts are paired by index and processed **one at a time** — this
  matches the pattern used by `ImageToTextPipeline` and avoids batch-size issues across
  heterogeneous VLM processors.
- A single text string is broadcast to all images in a batch.
- `this.processor(image, text)` delegates to the model-specific processor (e.g.
  `LlavaProcessor`, `PaliGemmaProcessor`) which handles image tokenisation + text tokenisation
  and their fusion internally.
- `this.tokenizer.batch_decode(output, { skip_special_tokens: true })` follows the exact same
  decoding pattern used by `ImageToTextPipeline`.
- Return type mirrors `ImageToTextPipeline`: `[{generated_text}]` for single input,
  `[[{generated_text}], ...]` for batched.

---

### Step 4 — Register in `SUPPORTED_TASKS` (insert after line 3255)

Add the new entry immediately after the `"image-to-text"` block (which closes at line 3255):

```js
"image-text-to-text": {
    "tokenizer": AutoTokenizer,
    "pipeline": ImageTextToTextPipeline,
    "model": AutoModelForImageTextToText,
    "processor": AutoProcessor,
    "default": {
        "model": "HuggingFaceTB/SmolVLM-256M-Instruct",
    },
    "type": "multimodal",
},
```

---

### Step 5 — Update the `pipeline()` JSDoc task list (around line 3427)

In the JSDoc comment for the `pipeline()` function (lines 3418–3440), add an entry to the
alphabetically-sorted list:

```
 *  - `"image-text-to-text"`: will return a `ImageTextToTextPipeline`.
```

Insert it between the `"image-to-text"` and `"object-detection"` entries, maintaining the
existing alphabetical order.

---

## Summary of All Insertions

| # | Location in `src/pipelines.js` | What |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Import block ~line 44 | Add `AutoModelForImageTextToText` to named imports from `./models.js` |
| 2 | After line 1953 | Add 3-typedef JSDoc block |
| 3 | After line 2005 | Add `ImageTextToTextPipeline` class (~30 lines) |
| 4 | After line 3255 | Add `"image-text-to-text"` entry in `SUPPORTED_TASKS` |
| 5 | ~line 3427 | Add one bullet to `pipeline()` JSDoc |

**No existing classes are modified.**

---

## Verification

1. **Type-check import:** Confirm `AutoModelForImageTextToText` is exported from `src/models.js`
   at line 8736 — it is: `export class AutoModelForImageTextToText extends PretrainedMixin`.

2. **Smoke test (manual):**
   ```js
   import { pipeline } from '@huggingface/transformers';
   const pipe = await pipeline('image-text-to-text', 'HuggingFaceTB/SmolVLM-256M-Instruct');
   const out = await pipe('https://...image.jpg', 'Describe this image.');
   console.log(out); // [{ generated_text: '...' }]
   ```

3. **Batched test:**
   ```js
   const out = await pipe(
     ['https://...img1.jpg', 'https://...img2.jpg'],
     ['What is in the first image?', 'What is in the second image?']
   );
   console.log(out); // [[{ generated_text: '...' }], [{ generated_text: '...' }]]
   ```

4. **Instance check:** `pipe instanceof ImageTextToTextPipeline` should be `true`.

5. **Run existing test suite** (if available):
   ```bash
   npm test
   ```

## Changes Made to `src/pipelines.js`

### 1. Import (line 44)
Added `AutoModelForImageTextToText` to the named import from `./models.js`.

### 2. JSDoc typedefs (after line 1953, now at lines 1957–1969)
Added three new types:
- `ImageTextToTextSingle` — `{ generated_text: string }`
- `ImageTextToTextOutput` — `ImageTextToTextSingle[]`
- `ImageTextToTextPipelineCallback` — the `_call` signature
- `ImageTextToTextPipelineType` — composite type for TypeScript consumers

### 3. `ImageTextToTextPipeline` class (lines 2022–2064)
The new pipeline class:
- Extends `Pipeline` via `TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs` (tokenizer + processor + model)
- `_call(images, text, generate_kwargs = {})` — accepts image(s) and text prompt(s)
- Normalises a single text string into an array broadcast across all images
- Processes each image+text pair through `this.processor(image, text)` (model-specific processor handles fusion)
- Calls `this.model.generate({ ...inputs, ...generate_kwargs })` for autoregressive generation
- Decodes output tokens via `this.tokenizer.batch_decode(output, { skip_special_tokens: true })`
- Returns `[{ generated_text }]` for single input; `[[{ generated_text }], ...]` for batched

### 4. `SUPPORTED_TASKS` entry (lines 3315–3324)
Registered `"image-text-to-text"` as a `"multimodal"` task with `AutoModelForImageTextToText`, `AutoTokenizer`, `AutoProcessor`, and default model `HuggingFaceTB/SmolVLM-256M-Instruct`.

### 5. `pipeline()` JSDoc list (line 3496)
Added `"image-text-to-text"` → `ImageTextToTextPipeline` to the supported task bullet list.

No existing pipeline classes were modified.
